question_text: A light bulb has a total energy input of 100 J. It produces 20 J of light energy and 80 J of heat energy. Calculate the efficiency of the light bulb. Show your working.
explanation: Efficiency = useful output / total input x 100. The useful output is the light energy (20 J), and the total input is 100 J. So efficiency = 20/100 x 100 = 20%. The 80 J of heat is wasted energy that does not produce light.
